info.name: IRCTC / RailBook — REST edge (design [D])
info.description: Buildable [D] REST contract for the IRCTC design in system-designs-with-deva/irctc. NOT the real (unpublished) IRCTC/CRIS API — the public edge you'd build, faithful to the on-screen decisions (REST edge, gRPC internal, GraphQL skipped; Admission-Token + Idempotency-Key on POST /book; 202-async allocation; pay-then-allocate + compensating refund). Set {{baseUrl}} to a mock server. See api-contracts.md.

request.description: Cacheable, seconds-stale. A rumour not a promise — you can read AVAILABLE and still land waitlist. Returns { status (AVAILABLE|RAC|WL|REGRET), availableCount/wlNumber, fare, stale, asOf }.

The waiting room. 200 ADMITTED returns { admissionToken, expiresAt, shard }; 202 QUEUED returns { queuePosition, pollUrl }. Token is bound to the shard (train:date) and short-lived. Meters admission to the rate the owners can commit.

header.description: proof you passed the gate — required

request.description: Pay-then-allocate: paymentRef proves money was captured first. 202 Accepted returns { bookingId, state: PENDING_ALLOCATION, statusUrl }. Allocation resolves async behind the single-writer shard owner. 403 without a valid Admission-Token; 409 on Idempotency-Key reuse with a different body.
Poll the async result — never re-POST /book. Returns { state (PENDING_ALLOCATION|CONFIRMED|RAC|WAITLIST|FAILED|CANCELLED|REFUNDED), pnr (10-digit), per-passenger status CNF|RAC|WL + coach/berth }.

Cancellation is itself a small financial transaction, so it is idempotent. 202 Accepted returns { state: CANCELLATION_PENDING, refund: { refundId, cancellationCharge, estimatedRefund, slaWorkingDays: 5, statutoryBasis: RBI TAT } }. A captured-but-unallocated payment is auto-refunded (saga compensation), legally capped by RBI TAT (T+5 working days).
